title: ECDH, X25519, and Authenticated Key Agreement · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Ecdh Contract
summary: A device protocol treats ECDH output as an encryption key, never authenticates peer keys, accepts malformed encodings, and reuses ephemeral keys indefinitely. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ecdh contract increment in authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Use supported ECDH curves through Go interfaces with private/public key separation, strict parsing, and shared-secret error handling.
Inventory RSA uses, migrate by purpose to modern approved alternatives, retain verified old-read windows, and remove obsolete private keys.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Ecdh Contract · Theory Crypto Go Ecdh Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 9904 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
